qas.q: JLV receives patient data from all of the following sources except:
Which statement is most true of the Commonly Used Widgets tab?
qas.a: C) It's just an example and should probably be customized to fit your workflow.

qas.q: Which of the following is not a clinical domain represented by it's own widget in JLV?
All legacy patient records can be found in MHS Genesis without necessarily having to access JLV.
Which is the symbol used to represent DOD data in JLV?
Which statement is not true of Date Range filters?
qas.a: C) The default date range for widgets is 5 years.

qas.q: How do you add a row of data and the available details to the Report Builder?
qas.a: B) Click the plus symbol in the left-most column.

qas.q: How do you choose a level of detail when adding data to the Report Builder?
Accessing sensitive data requires an audit acknowledge once per patient, per session.
What does an up arrow or down arrow in a column header mean?
qas.a: A) Column sorting has been applied to the column.

qas.q: When widgets results in a widget are paginated, selecting the plus icon in the column header on a widget selects all returned records, including those on other other results pages.
Which statement is not true of pre-set widget filters?
qas.a: B) Pre-set filters are the same for all widgets.

qas.q: Widgets can be configured to hide columns that are not of interest to the user.
Which of the following is not a customizable option that can improve your experience using JLV?
Effectively configuring JLV greatly increases its usefulness.
